rejected_candidates.title: Unsafe delegatecall path allows arbitrary wallet storage corruption
rejected_candidates.reason: `Bybit.sol` is a PoC that invokes an external Safe via `execTransaction`; it does not implement the victim wallet logic. Allowing owner-approved `DelegateCall` is an expected Safe capability, and this code shows an attack workflow that depends on externally obtained signatures rather than a missing on-chain authorization check in the audited source.

rejected_candidates.title: Slot-0 storage collision in Trojan replaces the proxy implementation
rejected_candidates.reason: `Trojan` is an explicitly malicious attacker helper contract included to demonstrate the exploit. Its slot-0 overwrite is intentional payload behavior, not an unintended vulnerability in the audited codebase.

rejected_candidates.title: Backdoor implementation exposes unrestricted ETH and token sweeping
rejected_candidates.reason: `Backdoor` is likewise an attacker-controlled drain contract in the PoC. Public sweep functions on a malicious contract are expected behavior and do not constitute a reportable issue in the audited source.
